title: Mailchimp vs MailerLite (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp vs MailerLite. See pricing, features, ratings & which email marketing tool is right for you.
intro: Mailchimp and MailerLite are both popular freemium email marketing platforms designed to help businesses build and manage email campaigns. While both offer drag-and-drop editors and automation, they differ in pricing, free tier limits, and specialized features. This comparison will help you decide which platform best fits your business needs.
choose_a_if: You need an all-in-one marketing platform with landing pages, CRM, and multi-channel capabilities
You want the established brand with 20+ years of market presence
You require advanced enterprise-level features and integrations
choose_b_if: You're budget-conscious and want lower starting prices ($9 vs $13)
You need a larger free tier (1,000 vs 500 subscribers)
You run an e-commerce store and need built-in shop integrations and pop-ups
You value customer reviews and support quality (4.5 vs 1.5 Trustpilot rating)

faq.a: Neither is objectively "better" — it depends on your needs. MailerLite offers superior value for budget-conscious users with lower prices, a larger free tier, and significantly higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.5 vs 1.5 on Trustpilot). Mailchimp excels for businesses needing an all-in-one platform with advanced enterprise features, though it has received more negative recent reviews.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or MailerLite?
faq.a: MailerLite is cheaper overall. It starts at $9/month compared to Mailchimp's $13/month, and offers double the free subscriber limit (1,000 vs 500). For growing teams, MailerLite's pricing structure typically remains more affordable across all tiers.

faq.q: Can I switch from MailerLite to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list exports and imports. You can export your contacts and campaign history from MailerLite and import them into Mailchimp. Most modern email marketing tools support CSV or API-based migrations, though you may lose some automation workflows that need to be recreated.
